author: 0xunclebill
content:
i have a few edge cases regarding payload attributes sse event and wondering if anyone else is coming across this. specifically serving the parent block number

edge case 1: if the non-finalized portion of the chain has no payload envelopes, we cant use fork choice to fetch the parent block number sice the latest finalized full block will have already been pruned from fc. we instead need to load it from the db by iterating back until we find the latest full block.

edge case 2: at the fork. at the fork block we fetch the parent block number from the pre-gloas block itself. if at the fork we have a missed payload envelope, we have to use fork choice to look at latest pre-gloas block to get the parent block number. and if we finalize gloas with zero envelopes, were back at edge case 1, but need some special logic to iterate back to the latest pre-gloas block.

probably other ways to solve this, but I think this is the most straightforward way to do it in lighthouse.

Its just feels like a lot of annoying edge cases to deal with. Do we really need to serve the parent block number? couldnt the EL derive that with just the parent block hash? it would make things easier for us. wondering what other clients think

author: 0xunclebill
content: oh michael wrote about this in the beacon api channel

author: 0xunclebill
content: sorry

author: 0xunclebill
content: still havent finished my coffee 😅

author: dgusakov
content: But why? Is there an intent from major builders to go all staked? Or do we think that manging direct builder connections is better than using relays?

author: tersec
content:
Yeah I'm actually a bit confused here. There are two different things
- P2P bids, etc. Sure yeah. Orthogonal.
- Potential future deprecation of the _builder API_ as a whole (might happen, might not, isn't for Gloas)
- And this `mev-boost` thing. Just, why?

author: 0xunclebill
content: I believe VC needs to sign a request auth per builder post Gloas. so mev boost would need direct access to VC keys in order to multiplex correctly

author: 0xunclebill
content:
probably mev boost could be updated to support that. but seeing as over at least the past 24months its pretty much just been core devs maintaining mev-boost i dont see the point in putting more work into it. 

commit boost supports remote signing so they could probs support post-gloas functionality

author: nc1234
content: I think it has been poorly maintained. And I think Bharath's (omg I can't tag him anymore he left the server) argument is that Prysm has the ability to connect to single relay, then it should be trivial for clients to connect to multiple relays which is really all mev boost does. So instead of updating mev boost, might as well just deprecate it and let clients write their own multiplexer

author: tersec
content: it's introducing a lot of random jank for something I keep seeing might be potentially deprecated by Hegota

author: tersec
content: it's easier to purge the jank if it's not part of CLs but some separate tool

author: sproul
content:
I think I broke the spec? https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/issues/5399

if I'm wrong though I'll buy you a beer 🍻

author: vladimir_ea
content: I have a stupid question: the spec says that builders should publish execution payloads on the global `execution_payload` p2p gossip topic. Is the expectation that builders are themselves maintaining p2p gossip connections? Or is there a new api on beacon nodes that can be called? thanks.

author: barnabasbusa
content: yes builders will have to run a beacon node

author: vladimir_ea
content: and what is the api that should be called to published execution payload?

author: nflaig
content: there will be an api to publish it, see [here](https://github.com/ethereum/beacon-APIs/pull/580/changes#diff-81ee70f2d3ef064583bf30a5dd912e21e004476334fb825d16e72bc2bd411e12), there is also open question if we need `broadcast_validation` there, somehting builders should chime in as well

author: barnabasbusa
content: this exact PR will be discussed probably on acdc today

author: nflaig
content: would be `POST /eth/v1/beacon/execution_payload_envelopes`

author: nflaig
content: regarding `broadcast_validation`, there is a good comment from Michael [here](https://github.com/ethereum/beacon-APIs/pull/580#discussion_r2887819954)

author: vladimir_ea
content: what guarantees can a 'check for equivocation' provide? if i understand, beacon block attestations are produced at the 3 second mark and are then propagated - so is it the case that a builder can only guarantee there is no equivocation by waiting to observe a majority of attestations before broadcasting the payload?

author: vladimir_ea
content: if this is the case then it would be useful to builders to be able ot query a beacon node to discover if this point has been reached, rather than repeatly send a payload to the beascon node until this point is reached.
